title: Data quality ratings for 2018 Census variables | Stats NZ
content:
## Data quality ratings for 2018 Census variables
23 September 2019, 10:00am
Data quality ratings for 2018 Census variables summarises the quality rating and priority levels for 2018 Census variables, such as age, sex, birthplace, and occupation.
25 May 2020: Languages spoken – typo in data quality rating fixed (was moderate, now high).
30 April 2020: We have updated data quality ratings for the following variables:
- absentees (was very poor, now moderate/very poor)
- families and households: extended family type (was very poor, now poor)
- families and households: family type (was very poor, now moderate)
- families and households: household composition (was very poor, now moderate)
- sources of family, extended family, and household income (new variable: moderate/poor)
- total family, extended family, and household income (new variable: moderate/poor) .
21 April 2020: We have updated data quality ratings for the following variables:
- number of rooms (was poor, now moderate)
- sector of ownership (was moderate, now high)
- status in employment (was moderate, now high)
- study participation (was high, now moderate)
- work and labour force status (was moderate, now high)
- years since arrival in New Zealand (was moderate, now high).
2018 Census External Data Quality Panel: Assessment of variables summarises the panel's findings.
- About the quality rating process
- Summary of quality ratings

## About the quality rating process
We evaluated each variable and assigned them an overall quality rating on a five-point scale ranging from very high to very poor. As a similar rating scale was used in the 2013 Census, researchers can compare data quality across censuses.
Data quality assurance for 2018 Census has more detail on the process.
We also assigned a priority level to each of the variables, to determine the relative importance of the variables.
2018 Census quality management strategy (Appendix 2) explains the three priority levels.

## Summary of quality ratings
The links in the table below go to detailed information about each variable, including definition, priority level in the quality management strategy, quality rating, subject population, question format, data sources, classification information, missing and residual response information, comparability with previous census data, data use, and any issues to be aware of relating to the variable.
2018 Census information by variable and quality – DataInfo+ also contains this information.
